residualList.sentence: He does not want to work.
Grandma closes the book and says that was our eighth night.

sample.before: My ears are pointed up and waiting.
sample.after: My ears stand straight up and wait.

sample.before: A big shadow makes her shiver in fear.
sample.after: A big shadow makes her shake all over.

sample.before: Her heart aches deep within her.
sample.after: Her heart aches deep inside her chest.

sample.before: Not a single person walks past the alley.
sample.after: Not one person walks past the alley.

sample.before: In order to reach the small furry one.
sample.after: In order to reach the little stray.

sample.before: She looks toward the ground.
sample.after: She looks at the ground below her.

sample.before: She takes out a gentle drying cloth.
sample.after: She takes out a clean piece of cloth.

sample.before: Soon the fur is without wet.
sample.after: Soon the fur is no longer damp.

sample.before: She cannot haul the small furry creature house.
sample.after: She cannot take the little stray with her.

sample.before: Her face is gentle-hearted again.
sample.after: Her face is warm and gentle again.

sample.before: Grandma says, "I have one more story for you."
sample.after: Grandma says, "I have just one more tale for you."

sample.before: The ant works very with strong effort.
sample.after: The ant works very, very long days.

sample.before: Grandma says, "One more story this evening."
sample.after: Grandma says, "Just one more tale this evening."

sample.before: Long ago, the north wind and the sun fell into a long talk-fight.
sample.after: Long ago, the north wind and the sun could not agree.

sample.before: The north wind pushes air hard very hard.
sample.after: The north wind sends cold air very, very fast.

sample.before: The wind is cold and with much force.
sample.after: The wind is cold and full of force.

sample.before: He wraps it on every side himself.
sample.after: He wraps it close to himself.

sample.before: Now the sun begins to send out bright rays.
sample.after: Now the sun begins to glow softly.

sample.before: A wish for sweet rest.
sample.after: A wish for sweet sleep.

sample.before: I walk to the rear slowly.
sample.after: I walk away slowly.

sample.before: Grandma has a new book in sky-toned.
sample.after: Grandma has a new book in deep sky color.

sample.before: The volume with the sky-toned cover.
sample.after: The volume with the deep sky cover.

sample.before: We both look at Grandma. She is about to set off on.
sample.after: We both look at Grandma. She is about to open the page.

sample.before: A small one of their own.
sample.after: A little one of their very own.

sample.before: It was flower-toned and round and very big.
sample.after: It was rose-colored and round and very big.

sample.before: The peach rode the water toward her hands.
sample.after: The peach drifted on the water toward her hands.

sample.before: The peach was with much weight in her arms.
sample.after: The peach was full of weight in her arms.

sample.before: Sitting up on the kitchen surface.
sample.after: Sitting up on the cooking top.

sample.before: A sharp blade with a handle.
sample.after: A sharp tool with a handle.

sample.before: He was with much force and fear-less.
sample.after: He was full of force and full of courage.
